text_draft:
Later, when the eggs were found and the hunt was over, Susie wrapped Pink Bear in a warm towel.
They sat together by the window. Outside, the snow still fell, soft and quiet.
Ruby shuffled over. The old terrier rested her gray head on Susie's lap, close to Pink Bear.
Three friends. One snowy Easter morning that didn't go as planned.
But turned out perfect anyway.
Pink Bear looked at the garden beyond the big bright window—no longer buried, no longer impossible.
And she smiled her silent smile.
Everything was exactly as it should be.
